7-Day Kruger National Park Safari Our Most Complete Big 5 Safari Experience

Unlike shorter safaris that focus on a single region, this 7-day Kruger National Park safari gives you time to slow down, explore multiple wildlife habitats, and revisit productive areas when sightings are at their best. By spending a full week inside the park, you maximise your opportunities to see the Big 5, enjoy exceptional birdlife, and experience Kruger at a relaxed pace without feeling rushed from one destination to the next.

Why Choose a 7-Day Kruger Safari?

Many first-time visitors wonder whether seven days in Kruger National Park is too long. In reality, a full week gives you something shorter safaris simply cannot offer: time.

Instead of rushing between camps and trying to fit everything into a few days, a 7-day safari allows you to slow down and experience the park at a natural pace. You have more opportunities to explore different habitats, revisit productive wildlife areas, and spend longer enjoying sightings without constantly watching the clock.

Kruger National Park covers nearly 20,000 square kilometres, making it one of Africa's largest wildlife destinations. Wildlife activity changes from day to day, and even experienced guides cannot predict exactly where lions, leopards, or wild dogs will appear. The advantage of a longer safari is that you have more game drives, more time in the bush, and more opportunities for memorable sightings.

This safari travels through several of Kruger's most rewarding wildlife regions, giving guests excellent opportunities to encounter the Big 5, cheetahs, wild dogs, giraffes, zebras, hyenas, hippos, crocodiles, and hundreds of bird species.

For wildlife enthusiasts, photographers, birders, and first-time safari travellers, seven days often provides the perfect balance between exploration, relaxation, and wildlife viewing.

Which Kruger Safari Is Right For You?

Every traveller is different. Whether you're short on time or looking for the ultimate wildlife experience, we have a safari to suit your pace.

Quick Escape

5-Day Safari

Perfect for travellers with limited time who still want an authentic Big 5 safari experience.

  • ✓ Best for first-time visitors
  • ✓ Excellent wildlife opportunities
  • ✓ Ideal with Cape Town add-ons
  • ✓ Lower overall cost

Best Balance

6-Day Safari

A balanced itinerary offering plenty of wildlife viewing while keeping travel time and budget in check.

  • ✓ Great value
  • ✓ More time in the bush
  • ✓ Multiple wildlife regions
  • ✓ Popular choice

Most Immersive Ultimate Experience

7-Day Safari

Our most complete safari experience, designed for travellers wanting the best opportunity to experience everything Kruger has to offer.

  • ✓ Maximum wildlife opportunities
  • ✓ Three distinct regions
  • ✓ Ideal for photographers
  • ✓ Relaxed safari pace
Enquire Now

Wildlife You Can Expect on a 7-Day Safari

One of the greatest advantages of a 7-day Kruger National Park safari is the amount of time you spend in the bush. Wildlife sightings are never guaranteed, but a full week allows us to explore multiple habitats, revisit productive areas, and maximise opportunities to encounter Kruger's most sought-after species. From the iconic Big 5 to endangered wild dogs and hundreds of bird species, every day brings the possibility of something new.

Compare Your Safari Options

Both 7 day Kruger National Park Safari packages feature the exact same route, expert private guiding, and authentic rest camp accommodation. Choose the level of hosting that suits your style.

Fully Hosted & Catered

All-Inclusive
  • ✓Accommodation: Kruger National Park Rest Camps
  • ✓Game Drives: Unlimited (Morning, Afternoon & Full-Day)
  • ✓Meals: All meals cooked & provided by your guide
  • ✓Park Fees: Included & managed entirely for you
  • ✓Drinks: Bottled water included throughout

Flexi-Safari

Guiding & Stay
  • ✓Accommodation: Kruger National Park Rest Camps
  • ✓Game Drives: Unlimited (Morning, Afternoon & Full-Day)
  • ✗Meals: Self-catered (Use camp kitchens or restaurants)
  • ✗Park Fees: Excluded (Paid directly to SANParks)
  • ✗Drinks: Excluded (Purchase at rest camp shops)

100% Flexible Flight & Route Logistics

Because we prioritize optimizing your time in the bush, this 7 Day Kruger national Park safari can be executed comfortably in either direction. Depending on your airline preferences and ticket availability, you can choose to land at Eastgate Airport (Hoedspruit) and depart via Skukuza Airport, or run the entire sequence perfectly in reverse. Your guide coordinates all regional airport transfers seamlessly.

7-Day Kruger Park Safari: The Itinerary

No matter which level of hosting you choose, you will share the exact same incredible routes, wildlife viewing tracks, private safaris and classic rest camp locations with your professional guide.

Camps 1 & 2 • Satara (2 Nights)

Days 1 - 2: Entry at Orpen Gate & The Plains of Satara

Your safari begins with your arrival at Eastgate Airport (Hoedspruit). After meeting your guide, we make the short drive to Orpen Gate to enter the park. The route toward Satara immediately introduces you to a variety of wildlife and then entering open savannah landscapes—renowned as the predator capital of Kruger due to the high density of lion prides, cheetahs, and hyenas that hunt in these wide-open spaces.

Over these two days, we will embark on early morning and late afternoon game drives along the famous S100 and Nwanetsi river roads, maximizing our chances of witnessing active big cat behavior.

Fully Hosted Tier

Your guide prepares classic bush breakfasts at picnic spots and handles traditional wood-fired braais under the stars back at the Satara communal cooking bomas.

Flexi-Safari Tier

Enjoy evening flexibility. Cook your own dinner on your bungalow’s private braai grid, or dine at the on-site Satara restaurant after your game drive.

Camps 3 & 4 • Skukuza (2 Nights)

Days 3 - 4: Down the Spine of Kruger to Skukuza

On Day 3, we pack up early and execute a slow, game-drive traverse heading south. As we move closer to the Sabie River system, the vegetation thickens into dense riverine woodlands—prime territory for the elusive leopard and massive herds of elephants.

Our home base for the next two nights is Skukuza Rest Camp, the historic heart of Kruger. From here, we patrol loops along the riverbanks where hippos wallow and crocodiles bask, providing fantastic visual framing for wildlife photography.

Fully Hosted Tier

All meals are seamlessly provided. Enjoy cooked meals at your bungalow, on drive at picnic sites managed entirely by your guide.

Flexi-Safari Tier

Take advantage of Skukuza’s extensive infrastructure. You can visit the Cattle Baron restaurant, grab snacks at the large park shop, or self-cater at your leisure.

Camps 5 & 6 • Berg-en-Dal (2 Nights)

Days 5 - 7: Granite Kopjes, Rhino Country & Skukuza Departure

We change environments completely on Day 5 as we venture to Berg-en-Dal Rest Camp, tucked away in the rugged, mountainous southwestern corner of the park. This area is characterized by striking granite hills (kopjes) and unique woodland flora, offering a completely different aesthetic for your photos. It is also an absolute stronghold for both white and black rhinos.

On Day 7, we run one final early morning safari drive as we make our way back toward the center of the southern block, dropping you off directly at Skukuza Airport for your flight out.

Fully Hosted Tier

Your final nights feature hearty, traditional campfire dinners prepared by your guide amidst the quiet, scenic hills of Berg-en-Dal.

Flexi-Safari Tier

Prepare meals at your leisure in the peaceful camp surroundings or visit the local camp cafeteria before packing for your departure flight.

Transparent Pricing

7-Day Lion Kruger National Park Safari Investment

Our vehicle accommodates up to 9 people, but we cap private group bookings strictly at 6 guests. This guarantees every single person has an outside row window seat with direct, completely unobstructed views—giving you full freedom of movement for professional-grade wildlife photography.

Fully Hosted & Catered Tier (USD Rates)

Private Couple (2 Guests)

Exclusive 4x4 use, private guide, 1 bungalow

$2,295 per person

Small Group (3–4 Guests)

Shared fixed costs, ideal for families, 2 bungalows

$1,895 per person
Best Value

Full Vehicle Group (5–6 Guests)

Maximized vehicle cost distribution, 3 bungalows

$1,595 per person

*Single Supplement applies to solo travelers requiring a private room. Flexi-Safari tier (Guiding & Stay only) prices available upon direct inquiry.

What Your Rate Covers Completely:

  • ✓ Six nights inside SANParks Rest Camps
  • ✓ All R602/day International Park Fees
  • ✓ Dedicated private safari vehicle & fuel
  • ✓ Daily custom-cooked meals & drinks
  • ✓ Hoedspruit & Skukuza Airport transfers
5 Days (Express) 6 Days (Southern Block) 7 Days (Grand Traverse)
Let's Tailor Your Trip
